  • Review Text - "When I sat in my car that day, fear twisting into nausea, frantically scrolling through an endless sea of lawyer ads. I had no idea who to trust. I chose Jeff. He looked like an angry guard dog—and in that moment, I needed that.

He guided me with expertise, calmness, and total honesty. Looking back on that version of me frantic, in my car, hiring Jeff Wolf was one of the best decisions I’ve ever made.
    And that angry guard dog look? It hides a genuinely warm and caring person."

Title: Sex offender deregistration 

Review: 
Colleen Kelly and her team, supported me and guided me through the process of getting off the registration . I had attempted over the years to do this on my own and at one point hired another attorney to assist me. These efforts were futile, I ended up with unnecessary years ( decades ) of suffering financially, humiliating encounters with personal relationships, landlords, employers, churches and other organizations.
Colleen and team were very professional and knew the steps that I needed to go through to get the job done. I would highly recommend Wolf Law, for their professionalism and caring.

Name: Alvin 

Thanks much!

title: Add videos to Court TV section of the site
description:
We have a couple videos to add to the Court TV section of the site!

https://wolflawcolorado.com/courttv/

---

Jeffrey Wolf Appears on Court TV to Comment on Forgetful Grandmother Case

In the tragic case of Tracey Nix, the forgetful grandmother our own Founding Partner Jeffrey Wolf went on Court TV to discuss the difficult task in front of the prosecution, defense team, and jury in determining guilty or innocence in such a unique case.  The jury returned a guilty verdict on a lesser charge of leaving a child in a vehicle, but found Ms. Nix not guilty of Manslaughter of her grandchild highlighting the difficulty Jeffrey was addressing with hosts Ashley Wilcott and Michael Ayala.

https://youtu.be/RqR5jq5h7sE

---

Jeffrey Wolf's In-Depth Analysis of the Forgetful Grandmother Case | Extended Court TV Discussion

Join criminal defense attorney Jeffrey Wolf as he appears on Court Tv, for an extended discussion on the heartbreaking Forgetful Grandmother Case. In this in-depth video, Jeff delves deeper into the legal and emotional complexities of the case, exploring the jurors' difficult task of balancing compassion with the standards of reasonable behavior and culpable negligence. He provides a comprehensive analysis of potential defense strategies, including the grandmother's mental state and medication factors. Don't miss this thorough examination of a tragic and thought-provoking case.

https://youtu.be/t9CCgTHUzOo

---

Jeffrey Wolf Discusses the Forgetful Grandmother Case on Court TV | What is Reckless? What is Negligence?
 
Founding Partner Jeffrey Wolf discussed how hard it would be for the jury to figure out whether Tracey Nix had acted with recklessness or negligence in the tragic death of her own grandchild that she left in her car for hours.  In fact, the jury ended up convicting her of a lesser offense and acquitting her of manslaughter in this extremely tragic death.

https://youtu.be/ngjzJ8IeqnA

---

Thank you!!
